verses.text: 'A false balance is abomination to the LORD,' but a just weight His delight; pride brings shame, and the integrity of the upright guides them. God watches our dealings. Honesty in business and humility of heart are precious in His sight.

'A false balance is abomination to the LORD,' but a just weight His delight; pride brings shame, and the integrity of the upright guides them. God watches our dealings. Honesty in business and humility of heart are precious in His sight.

'A false balance is abomination to the LORD,' but a just weight His delight; pride brings shame, and the integrity of the upright guides them. God watches our dealings. Honesty in business and humility of heart are precious in His sight.

Riches profit not in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death; the upright are saved by their integrity, the treacherous trapped by their own sin. Character outlasts wealth. Only righteousness, not riches, avails in the day of reckoning.

Riches profit not in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death; the upright are saved by their integrity, the treacherous trapped by their own sin. Character outlasts wealth. Only righteousness, not riches, avails in the day of reckoning.

Riches profit not in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death; the upright are saved by their integrity, the treacherous trapped by their own sin. Character outlasts wealth. Only righteousness, not riches, avails in the day of reckoning.

When a wicked man dies his hope perishes; the righteous is delivered from trouble, but the hypocrite would destroy his neighbour with his mouth. Words can wound or save. The godly are rescued, while the deceitful ensnare others by their speech.

When a wicked man dies his hope perishes; the righteous is delivered from trouble, but the hypocrite would destroy his neighbour with his mouth. Words can wound or save. The godly are rescued, while the deceitful ensnare others by their speech.

When a wicked man dies his hope perishes; the righteous is delivered from trouble, but the hypocrite would destroy his neighbour with his mouth. Words can wound or save. The godly are rescued, while the deceitful ensnare others by their speech.

A city rejoices at the good man's welfare and the wicked's fall; the void of wisdom despises his neighbour, but a man of understanding holds his peace. Wisdom is shown in restraint. The wise guard their tongue rather than belittle others.

A city rejoices at the good man's welfare and the wicked's fall; the void of wisdom despises his neighbour, but a man of understanding holds his peace. Wisdom is shown in restraint. The wise guard their tongue rather than belittle others.

A city rejoices at the good man's welfare and the wicked's fall; the void of wisdom despises his neighbour, but a man of understanding holds his peace. Wisdom is shown in restraint. The wise guard their tongue rather than belittle others.

A talebearer reveals secrets, but a faithful spirit conceals; without counsel people fall, and rash surety brings trouble. Faithfulness keeps confidence and seeks counsel. The wise are trustworthy with secrets and cautious in commitments.

A talebearer reveals secrets, but a faithful spirit conceals; without counsel people fall, and rash surety brings trouble. Faithfulness keeps confidence and seeks counsel. The wise are trustworthy with secrets and cautious in commitments.

A gracious woman keeps honour, the merciful man does good to his own soul, while the cruel troubles his own flesh; 'to him that soweth righteousness shall be a sure reward.' Kindness blesses the giver. What we sow in righteousness yields a certain harvest.

As righteousness tends to life, so the pursuer of evil to death; the froward are abomination to the LORD, but the upright His delight; the wicked shall not go unpunished. Two paths, two ends. God hates crookedness and rewards the blameless.

As righteousness tends to life, so the pursuer of evil to death; the froward are abomination to the LORD, but the upright His delight; the wicked shall not go unpunished. Two paths, two ends. God hates crookedness and rewards the blameless.

Beauty without discretion is 'as a jewel of gold in a swine's snout'; the righteous desire only good; one scatters yet increases, while another withholds and comes to want. Generosity enriches. God blesses the open hand and impoverishes the grasping.

'The liberal soul shall be made fat,' and he that waters others is watered himself; he who seeks good finds favour, but mischief comes to him who seeks it. Kindness returns to the kind. To bless others is to be blessed in turn.

'The liberal soul shall be made fat,' and he that waters others is watered himself; he who seeks good finds favour, but mischief comes to him who seeks it. Kindness returns to the kind. To bless others is to be blessed in turn.

'The liberal soul shall be made fat,' and he that waters others is watered himself; he who seeks good finds favour, but mischief comes to him who seeks it. Kindness returns to the kind. To bless others is to be blessed in turn.

He that trusts in riches shall fall, but the righteous flourish like a branch; he that troubles his house inherits wind, and 'the fruit of the righteous is a tree of life.' Trust rightly placed bears fruit. Wealth fails, but a life rooted in God flourishes and wins souls.
